name: ReadMe API
what_it_does: ReadMe provides a REST API for programmatically managing developer hubs including API reference pages, guides, changelogs, custom pages, and developer usage metrics.
use_cases: Automatically publish a new changelog entry whenever a new API version is deployed to production
Sync an updated OpenAPI spec to ReadMe from a CI/CD pipeline on every release branch merge
Query developer API usage metrics to identify which endpoints are most called and by whom
Programmatically create or update guide pages in response to content management system events
Retrieve and aggregate per-user API call logs to generate usage reports for enterprise customers
not_for: Building interactive application UIs or serving application data to end users
Managing non-developer-facing content like marketing copy or internal wikis
Real-time event streaming or WebSocket-based communication patterns
best_when: Best when you need a developer hub that combines API reference, guides, and per-user metrics in one platform with automation hooks for keeping content current.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need fully custom doc site layouts or when the ReadMe branding on the free tier is unacceptable for your use case.

auth.notes: API key passed via HTTP Basic Auth with the key as the username and an empty password. Keys are project-scoped and available from the ReadMe dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available with ReadMe branding visible on the docs site and limited to one project.
pricing.paid_tiers: Startup plan from ~$99/month; Business and Enterprise plans with custom pricing for white-labeling, SSO, and advanced metrics.
pricing.notes: Free tier is suitable for small open-source projects; branding removal requires a paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: 429 responses are returned when limits are hit; specific limits are not publicly documented but are permissive for typical CI/CD batch operations.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include a descriptive message field and an error code string, though some 400-level errors lack enough detail to distinguish root causes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: OpenAPI spec uploads using PUT are idempotent; doc page creation with POST is not and can create duplicates if retried without checking first.
known_agent_gotchas: API spec uploads process asynchronously; the upload endpoint returns 200 before content is live, requiring polling for readiness
Changelog entries require a specific slug format; slug collisions silently overwrite existing entries without returning a conflict error
The per-user metrics API requires a JWT signed with your project secret to associate API calls with specific developer identities
Category and guide hierarchies must be established before pages can be created; attempting to create an orphaned page returns a confusing 404
Version identifiers in URLs must exactly match the version string configured in the project, including v-prefix conventions

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTP Basic Auth with an API key as the username is an older pattern that works but lacks scope control. Keys cannot be rotated or scoped per operation type.
